feedback_endpoint.reason: No auth/PII/money. External surface (new HTTP endpoint) but low-risk data (memory IDs + outcome enum). Standard request validation already exists in /api/reinforce pattern.

weight_adaptation.reason: Complex state: TWO separate LearnedWeights systems (relevance.rs 7-dim, hybrid_search.rs 3-dim) with independent EMA updates. Novel tech: real-time gradient descent on scoring weights with no rollback mechanism. High coupling: weights affect ALL retrieval and ALL proactive surfacing.

bayesian_confidence_integration.reason: Already exists: MemoryMetadata.calibrated_confidence with alpha/beta tracking. Proposal extends usage, doesn't introduce new infrastructure. Medium coupling to retrieval scoring.

attribution_system.reason: Novel tech: decomposing a fused score into per-dimension contributions through 15+ additive boost layers. High coupling: must instrument the entire scoring pipeline. No existing precedent in codebase.

edge.data_crossing: Feedback signal → LearnedWeights update. But WHICH LearnedWeights? relevance.rs::LearnedWeights (7-dim, used only in surface_relevant) and hybrid_search.rs::LearnedWeights (3-dim, used in recall pipeline). These are independent structs with no shared state.
failure_modes: CRITICAL: Feedback to the proactive engine (relevance.rs) has ZERO effect on the recall pipeline (hybrid_search.rs), and vice versa. A user says 'helpful' via /api/reinforce → hybrid_search weights update → proactive surfacing is unaware of this signal. The two systems can DIVERGE: proactive engine learns to suppress a memory while recall engine learns to promote it.

CRITICAL: The 7-dim weights in relevance.rs are used ONLY in surface_relevant_inner (proactive surfacing). They are NEVER used in semantic_retrieve (the main recall path). The proposal's 'adjust the 7 scoring weights in real-time' targets a system that doesn't affect main retrieval.

HIGH: relevance.rs::apply_feedback takes boolean flags (semantic_contributed, entity_contributed, tag_contributed) but these are never computed from actual retrieval results. The method exists but has no callers in the recall pipeline — it's only used in tests.
edge.contracts: No shared interface. relevance.rs::LearnedWeights and hybrid_search.rs::LearnedWeights are unrelated types despite identical names.

fractal.pattern: Both weight systems implement the same concept: EMA-based weight adaptation from feedback. Both normalize to sum to 1.0. Both have learning rate constants.
fractal.mirror_test: BREAKS — same concept, different implementations, different dimensionality (7 vs 3), different update APIs (apply_feedback with booleans vs apply_update with SignalScores). Not a refactoring gap — a design contradiction.
fractal.breaks: The fractal breaks at the architectural level: a unified feedback-to-weight pipeline should have ONE weight space, not two.

hazop.NO_feedback_reaches_recall: Current state: /api/reinforce calls reinforce_recall which updates Bayesian confidence and Hebbian edges but NOT hybrid_search LearnedWeights. The weight learning path is disconnected from the feedback path.
hazop.MORE_weight_systems: Proposal adds a THIRD pathway (/api/feedback) without unifying the existing two. Three feedback paths, two weight systems, zero synchronization.
hazop.REVERSE_divergence: Proactive engine suppresses a memory (negative momentum) while recall engine promotes it (positive graph reinforcement). User gets contradictory behavior.

fmea.failure: Weight systems diverge, producing inconsistent ranking between proactive surfacing and explicit recall for the same memory
fmea.mitigation: Unify into single ScoringWeights with per-subsystem views (proactive reads 7-dim slice, hybrid reads 3-dim slice, both write to same store)

E1_dual_weight_incoherence.verdict: BLOCKER. The proposal builds on a split-brain architecture. Adding more feedback without unifying weights makes the incoherence worse, not better.

edge.data_crossing: Retrieval result → attribution → weight update. But the retrieval result loses its component breakdown before reaching any feedback handler.
failure_modes: HIGH: with_unified_score closure (recall.rs:2027) computes base_score + recency_boost + arousal_boost + credibility_boost + temporal_boost + feedback_multiplier. These components are SUMMED into a single f32 and the breakdown is DISCARDED. No attribution trace survives to the feedback layer.

HIGH: relevance.rs::apply_feedback takes boolean flags (semantic_contributed, entity_contributed, tag_contributed) — a 3-bit attribution for a 7-dimensional weight space. The remaining 4 dimensions (momentum, access_count, graph_strength, importance) receive only a blanket aux_delta = lr * 0.5 * direction. This is not attribution; it's uniform noise.

MEDIUM: hybrid_search.rs::SignalScores (bm25_contribution, vector_contribution, graph_contribution) IS proper attribution for the 3-dim space. But it's only computed inside hybrid_search and not propagated to the recall response or feedback handlers.
edge.contracts: SignalScores exists for 3-dim; nothing analogous exists for 7-dim. Need RetrievalAttribution struct capturing all scoring dimensions.

fractal.pattern: The feedback system (FeedbackStore) has rich attribution: entity overlap, semantic similarity, tool usage, information-theoretic attribution. But this attribution is for IMPLICIT feedback on proactive surfacing. The EXPLICIT feedback path (/api/reinforce) has zero attribution.
fractal.mirror_test: BREAKS — implicit path has 7+ signal types, explicit path has 3 boolean flags. Asymmetric attribution depth.

hazop.PART_OF_attribution: Only semantic/entity/tag booleans → 3-bit resolution for 7 continuous dimensions.
hazop.OTHER_THAN_gradient: Could use bandit-style exploration (UCB1) instead of gradient descent. Avoids attribution problem entirely by treating weight selection as an explore/exploit problem.

fmea.failure: Weight updates are noise-dominated because attribution is too coarse (3 bits for 7 dimensions), causing random walk instead of convergence
fmea.mitigation: Capture per-dimension contribution ratios during scoring (they're computed but discarded). Pass through to feedback layer as continuous floats, not booleans.

E2_attribution_vacuum.verdict: HIGH gap. Attribution infrastructure exists in fragments (SignalScores for 3-dim, implicit signals for proactive). Must be unified and extended to 7-dim before weight learning can converge.

edge.data_crossing: User action → explicit feedback signal. Most recall operations produce NO explicit feedback. The implicit feedback pipeline fires ONLY on proactive_context calls (hooks), not on recall.
failure_modes: HIGH: ADAPTIVE_WEIGHT_CONFIDENCE_THRESHOLD = 10 (constants.rs:1061). With explicit-only feedback, reaching 10 updates could take weeks. Weights remain at defaults for extended periods, making the entire adaptation system effectively inert.

MEDIUM: Implicit feedback (FeedbackStore) fires per proactive_context cycle (~every message in a Claude Code session). But explicit /api/reinforce is called rarely. The proposal assumes explicit feedback is the primary signal — this contradicts usage patterns.
MEDIUM: ADAPTIVE_WEIGHT_MAX_BLEND = 0.6 means even at full confidence, 40% of weights are still static defaults. This is a safety net but also limits the system's ability to adapt.
edge.contracts: No contract ensures minimum feedback rate. System must be correct with zero feedback (static defaults) and progressively improve with feedback.

fractal.pattern: The Bayesian confidence (calibrated_confidence) already handles sparsity correctly: alpha=1, beta=1 prior → 0.5 confidence → linear ramp to learned estimate. This is the RIGHT pattern.
fractal.mirror_test: HOLDS for per-memory adaptation. BREAKS for global weight adaptation: the 7-dim weights have no Bayesian prior, just raw gradient descent from arbitrary initialization.

hazop.LATE_feedback: Feedback arrives minutes/hours after retrieval. By then, context has shifted. Attribution to specific dimensions may be stale.
hazop.NO_feedback: Most common case. System must degrade gracefully to static defaults. Currently: hybrid_search does this correctly (effective_weights returns static when update_count=0). Relevance.rs does NOT — uses raw learned weights with no confidence gate.

E3_feedback_sparsity.verdict: MEDIUM risk. Sparsity is inherent and well-understood. The Bayesian per-memory path handles it correctly. The global weight path needs the same treatment (confidence ramp). The proposal should lean into implicit signals (already flowing) rather than depending on explicit feedback.

edge.data_crossing: Bad weights → poor retrieval → negative feedback → weights get worse → even poorer retrieval. Classic degenerate cycle.
failure_modes: HIGH: WEIGHT_LEARNING_RATE = 0.05, MIN_WEIGHT = 0.05. One negative feedback on a weight at 0.10 drops it to 0.05 (the floor). Two more negatives and it stays floored permanently. No recovery mechanism.

HIGH: FeedbackMomentum.update() has bidirectional acceleration (line 598-601): strong negative signals (confidence > 0.8, value < -0.3) BYPASS inertia and slam EMA directly to signal value. If this momentum feeds into weight updates, a single bad interaction can destabilize the entire weight vector.
MEDIUM: No circuit breaker or anomaly detection. If all 7 weights converge to MIN_WEIGHT (0.05 each), the system produces effectively random ranking.
edge.contracts: Missing: weight stability invariant. Should enforce: no single feedback event can change any weight by more than X%. Should enforce: weight entropy (Shannon) must stay above minimum threshold.

fractal.pattern: FeedbackMomentum has excellent stability safeguards: inertia (0.20-0.95 by type), age factor (0.8-1.1), history factor (0.7-1.1), stability factor (0.8-1.2). These are per-MEMORY safeguards.
fractal.mirror_test: BREAKS — per-memory momentum has 4 stability mechanisms. Per-weight learning has ZERO stability mechanisms. The weight update is naked gradient descent.
fractal.breaks: The safeguard architecture is asymmetric: sophisticated for per-memory adaptation, absent for global weight adaptation.

hazop.REVERSE_weights: Adversary sends N 'harmful' signals → all weights collapse to MIN_WEIGHT → system produces random ranking. Recovery requires manual weight reset.
hazop.MORE_acceleration: Bidirectional acceleration from FeedbackMomentum amplifies single-event impact on weights.

fmea.failure: Cascading weight collapse from sustained negative feedback causing retrieval quality death spiral
fmea.mitigation: Apply the FeedbackMomentum stability model to weight updates: inertia, confidence ramp, max delta per update, minimum entropy invariant

E4_negative_feedback_loop.verdict: HIGH risk. The asymmetry between per-memory stability (excellent) and per-weight stability (nonexistent) is the primary design failure. Must be addressed before shipping.

edge.data_crossing: /api/reinforce already accepts {ids, user_id, outcome: helpful|misleading|neutral}. Proposed /api/feedback adds {memory_id, query_id, outcome: helpful|irrelevant|harmful}. Two endpoints, overlapping semantics, different schemas.
failure_modes: MEDIUM: Client confusion — which endpoint to call? /api/reinforce for Hebbian strengthening, /api/feedback for weight learning? The distinction is internal implementation detail that should be hidden from the API consumer.
LOW: Maintaining two feedback codepaths doubles testing surface and doubles the risk of inconsistent state updates.
edge.contracts: API should have ONE feedback endpoint that triggers ALL internal feedback mechanisms (Bayesian, Hebbian, weight learning, momentum).

fractal.pattern: Single-endpoint-multiple-effects. The existing /api/reinforce already triggers reinforce_recall which does Bayesian + Hebbian. Should extend, not duplicate.
fractal.mirror_test: HOLDS — /api/remember is one endpoint that triggers storage + indexing + graph + embeddings. /api/reinforce should be one endpoint that triggers all feedback mechanisms.

E5_api_surface_duplication.verdict: LOW risk but poor design. Extend /api/reinforce with 'harmful' outcome and wire it to weight learning. Do not create /api/feedback.

phase_2_fractal_atam.core_pattern: Feedback-to-adaptation loop: observe retrieval outcome → attribute to scoring dimensions → update weights → affect next retrieval
phase_2_fractal_atam.macro_consistency: The codebase has this pattern for per-MEMORY adaptation (FeedbackMomentum → EMA → momentum score in retrieval). Missing for GLOBAL weight adaptation across retrieval dimensions.
phase_2_fractal_atam.meso_consistency: The two weight systems (7-dim proactive, 3-dim hybrid) both implement EMA adaptation but from different feedback sources and at different granularity. Structurally isomorphic but operationally disconnected.
phase_2_fractal_atam.micro_consistency: Individual components are well-implemented: Bayesian confidence tracking, momentum with inertia, trend detection via linear regression, context fingerprinting. The micro implementations are strong; the macro wiring is broken.

breaks.description: Two LearnedWeights types with identical purpose, different dimensionality, no synchronization. Adding a third feedback path amplifies rather than resolves this.

Per-memory adaptation has 4 stability mechanisms (inertia, age, history, stability factors). Global weight adaptation has 0 stability mechanisms in relevance.rs and only confidence ramp in hybrid_search.rs.
Implicit feedback has 7+ signal types with continuous attribution. Explicit feedback has 3 boolean flags. The systems that need the most attribution have the least.

implicit_vs_explicit.stimulus: Should weight adaptation come from implicit signals (already flowing) or explicit feedback (sparse but precise)?
implicit_vs_explicit.response: Implicit signals are high-volume, low-precision. Explicit feedback is low-volume, high-precision. The system needs both, with confidence weighting.
implicit_vs_explicit.tradeoff: Implicit-heavy means faster adaptation but more noise. Explicit-heavy means slower adaptation but more accurate. Current architecture accidentally chose: implicit for proactive, explicit for recall, neither for unified weights.

adaptation_speed_vs_stability.stimulus: WEIGHT_LEARNING_RATE = 0.05 means fast adaptation. But fast adaptation with sparse feedback = instability.
adaptation_speed_vs_stability.response: Lower learning rate or add confidence gating. The Bayesian approach (alpha/beta) naturally solves this — more observations → more confidence → more weight on learned values.
adaptation_speed_vs_stability.tradeoff: Current: fast but unstable (relevance.rs), slow but safe (hybrid_search.rs with ramp). Need: unified system that starts safe and accelerates with evidence.

phase_2_5_adversarial_challenge.note: Challenging all component scores >= 7.0 from Phase 1

E5_api_duplication_6_5.challenge: Score of 6.5 means low risk. But the duplication could cause a subtle bug: client calls /api/feedback for weight learning + /api/reinforce for Hebbian. Both modify the same memory's confidence from different angles. Is this a race condition?
E5_api_duplication_6_5.evidence: reinforce_recall takes write lock on memory metadata for Bayesian update. If /api/feedback also updates calibrated_confidence, they contend on the same lock. However, RocksDB serializes writes per-key, so the race is benign — last writer wins, both increments are valid.
E5_api_duplication_6_5.verdict: Score holds at 6.5. No race condition, but API confusion remains.

E3_sparsity_5_8.challenge: Score of 5.8 assumes implicit signals compensate for explicit sparsity. But the proposal is specifically about EXPLICIT feedback. If implicit signals already work, why add explicit?
E3_sparsity_5_8.evidence: Implicit signals work for proactive surfacing (momentum EMA). They do NOT work for the recall pipeline (no feedback path). The proposal's real value is connecting the recall pipeline to ANY feedback, not specifically explicit feedback. The explicit endpoint is a means, not the end.
E3_sparsity_5_8.verdict: Score reduced to 5.5. The proposal's framing around explicit feedback obscures the real need: connecting recall to the existing implicit feedback infrastructure.

correctness.evidence_code: LearnedWeights::apply_feedback (relevance.rs:427) correctly implements gradient descent with min floor and normalization. hybrid_search.rs::apply_update correctly implements EMA with normalization. Both are individually correct.
correctness.evidence_structural: But applying correct updates to the WRONG weight system produces incorrect behavior. Proactive weights adapt while recall weights don't — the feature's stated goal (adjust scoring weights in real-time for retrieval) cannot be achieved through the proposed architecture.

robustness.evidence_code: MIN_WEIGHT = 0.05, WEIGHT_LEARNING_RATE = 0.05. One negative signal drops a 0.10 weight to floor. No inertia, no confidence ramp on relevance.rs path.
robustness.evidence_structural: FeedbackMomentum has 4 stability mechanisms. LearnedWeights has 0. Bidirectional acceleration (feedback.rs:598) can slam momentum to -1.0 on a single strong negative, which if fed to weight updates could cascade.

security.evidence_code: Existing validation patterns in handlers (validate_user_id, UUID parsing). New endpoint would follow same pattern. No PII exposure — feedback is memory_id + outcome enum.
security.evidence_structural: Rate limiting exists on other endpoints. Weight manipulation via crafted feedback is a DoS vector (collapse weights to MIN_WEIGHT) but requires authenticated access.

performance.evidence_code: Weight reads are behind RwLock (concurrent reads OK). Weight updates are rare (explicit feedback only) so write contention is negligible. FeedbackStore 3-phase locking pattern (recall.rs:991-1112) minimizes lock hold time.
performance.evidence_structural: No hot path impact — weight reads are a single RwLock::read() returning f32 values. Attribution tracking adds ~5 field copies per scored memory in the hot path, sub-microsecond.

maintainability.evidence_code: Two LearnedWeights types with identical names in different modules. New contributor would not know which one to update. Tests in relevance.rs test only the 7-dim weights; tests in hybrid_search.rs test only the 3-dim weights. No integration test verifies end-to-end feedback flow.
maintainability.evidence_structural: Adding a third feedback pathway (/api/feedback) without unifying the weight systems increases maintenance burden from 2 to 3 independent feedback codepaths.

scalability.evidence_code: Bayesian confidence per-memory scales linearly with memory count (2 extra f32 per memory, already stored in MemoryMetadata). Global weights are constant-size (7 or 10 f32 values).
scalability.evidence_structural: No per-query state growth. No new storage requirements beyond existing RocksDB column families.

completeness.evidence_code: Missing: attribution computation in recall pipeline. Missing: confidence ramp on relevance.rs weights. Missing: weight unification. Missing: circuit breaker for weight collapse. The proposal adds a new endpoint but does not address the 4 critical missing pieces.
completeness.evidence_structural: The proposal builds the roof (feedback endpoint) before fixing the foundation (unified weights, attribution, stability). The existing /api/reinforce already provides the endpoint surface.

testability.evidence_code: Existing tests: relevance.rs has 8 tests for LearnedWeights including apply_feedback, normalize. hybrid_search.rs has 4 tests for LearnedWeights EMA convergence. FeedbackStore has comprehensive unit tests.
testability.evidence_structural: No integration test verifies: feedback → weight update → changed retrieval ranking. Would need a test that stores memories, retrieves, sends feedback, retrieves again, verifies ranking changed.

data_integrity.evidence_code: Attribution is 3-bit (boolean flags) for a 7-dimensional continuous space. Weight updates from this attribution are essentially noise. The aux_delta = lr * direction * 0.5 applied uniformly to momentum/access_count/graph_strength dimensions is not data-driven; it's a constant perturbation.
data_integrity.evidence_structural: The proposal assumes per-dimension attribution exists. It does not. Building weight adaptation on non-existent attribution produces garbage updates that corrupt the weight state over time.
data_integrity.blocker_note: Score 3.5 < 5.0 threshold. BLOCKER on Data Integrity.

coherence.evidence_code: Two weight systems (relevance.rs 7-dim, hybrid_search.rs 3-dim) with no shared type, no shared update mechanism, no synchronization. FeedbackMomentum updates are orthogonal to both.
coherence.evidence_structural: Three conceptual layers of adaptation (per-memory momentum, per-memory Bayesian, global weights) with no unified architecture connecting them. The proposal adds a 4th (explicit per-retrieval feedback) without addressing the existing fragmentation.
coherence.blocker_note: Score 3.8 < 5.0 threshold. BLOCKER on Coherence (grouped with Data Integrity as critical dimension).

documentation.evidence_code: Existing doc comments are thorough: FeedbackMomentum has full doc comments on every field and method. LearnedWeights has doc comments on all constants. RelevanceEngine methods are well-documented.
documentation.evidence_structural: No architectural documentation explaining the relationship (or lack thereof) between the two weight systems. A new developer reading the code would discover the split-brain by accident.

compliance.evidence_code: No external data sharing. Feedback stays in local RocksDB. No new PII collection. Memory IDs are UUIDs with no user-identifiable information.
compliance.evidence_structural: The feedback loop is entirely local — no cloud calls, no telemetry, no data export. Clean from a compliance perspective.

blockers: Robustness (3.8): No stability safeguards on global weight updates. Single negative event can floor a weight with no recovery.
Data Integrity (3.5): Attribution is 3-bit for 7-dim space. Weight updates are noise-dominated.
Coherence (3.8): Two independent weight systems, no unification path, proposal adds complexity without resolving fragmentation.

quality.rationale: Individual components are well-implemented (FeedbackMomentum, Bayesian tracking) but the system-level architecture has structural contradictions (dual weights, missing attribution).

completeness.rationale: Missing: unified weights, attribution tracking, stability safeguards, confidence ramp on proactive weights. The proposal addresses ~30% of what's needed.

performance.rationale: No hot-path impact. Weight reads are cached f32 behind RwLock. Attribution tracking adds sub-microsecond overhead per scored memory.

utility.rationale: Adaptive weights could improve retrieval quality over time. But with feedback sparsity and no attribution, the adaptation is slow and imprecise. May take months to observe meaningful weight shifts.

coherence.rationale: Two weight systems + three feedback pathways + no synchronization = architectural incoherence. Proposal amplifies rather than resolves.

cohesiveness.rationale: The per-memory adaptation layer (momentum, Bayesian, Hebbian) is cohesive. The global weight layer is fractured. The connection between the two layers is absent.

elasticity.rationale: RocksDB storage, constant-size weight vectors, linear scaling with memory count. No scalability concerns. But the fragmented architecture makes extension harder — adding a new scoring dimension requires changes in 2 weight types.

function.rationale: The feature's core function (close the feedback loop for retrieval weights) cannot be achieved as proposed because the 7-dim weights don't affect the recall pipeline. The 3-dim weights that DO affect recall have a separate, already-functional feedback path.

ease_of_use.rationale: API surface is clean (POST endpoint with simple JSON). But two feedback endpoints (/api/reinforce and /api/feedback) create confusion about which to use when.

what_the_proposal_misses.title: The 7-dim weights target the WRONG scoring path
what_the_proposal_misses.detail: The proposal says 'adjust the 7 scoring weights in real-time.' These 7 weights (semantic, entity, tag, importance, momentum, access_count, graph_strength) live in relevance.rs::LearnedWeights and are used ONLY in surface_relevant_inner() for PROACTIVE surfacing. They have ZERO effect on semantic_retrieve() which is the main recall pipeline. The main recall pipeline uses a completely different scoring architecture: base_score + recency_boost + arousal_boost + credibility_boost + temporal_boost, weighted by constants, not by LearnedWeights. To affect recall quality, you need to update hybrid_search.rs::LearnedWeights (3-dim: bm25, vector, graph) — which already has its own EMA feedback mechanism via update_weights_from_feedback().
what_the_proposal_misses.evidence: grep 'learned_weights' in src/memory/recall.rs returns 0 results. The recall pipeline never touches relevance.rs::LearnedWeights.

what_the_proposal_misses.title: apply_feedback() is dead code for the recall path
what_the_proposal_misses.detail: relevance.rs::RelevanceEngine::apply_feedback() (line 664) is only called from surface_relevant flow and tests. It is NEVER called from any recall handler, reinforce handler, or feedback processing path. The proposal assumes this method connects to the retrieval pipeline — it does not.
what_the_proposal_misses.evidence: All callers of apply_feedback: relevance.rs tests (lines 1895, 1923, 2011). Zero callers in handlers/, memory/, or anywhere outside test code.

what_the_proposal_misses.title: Existing /api/reinforce already does 80% of the proposal
what_the_proposal_misses.detail: The proposal wants: (1) feedback ingestion endpoint, (2) per-memory confidence update, (3) edge strengthening/weakening, (4) weight learning. /api/reinforce already does (1), (2), and (3). It accepts {ids, user_id, outcome: helpful|misleading|neutral}, updates Bayesian confidence (reinforce_recall → update_confidence), and strengthens/weakens Hebbian edges. The only missing piece is (4) weight learning — which is a one-line addition (call update_weights_from_feedback after reinforce_recall). Creating a new /api/feedback endpoint duplicates 80% of existing work.
what_the_proposal_misses.evidence: handlers/recall.rs:2332-2411 implements the full reinforcement pipeline.

what_the_proposal_misses.title: Implicit feedback already flows — and works
what_the_proposal_misses.detail: The proposal focuses on EXPLICIT feedback (user clicks helpful/harmful). But the implicit feedback pipeline is already operational: proactive_context handler (recall.rs:982) processes previous_response, computes entity overlap / semantic similarity / tool usage / information-theoretic attribution / negative keywords / repetition detection / topic change, and updates FeedbackMomentum accordingly. This feeds into retrieval via feedback_multiplier (recall.rs:2089). The implicit pipeline is richer, higher-volume, and already connected. The explicit pipeline is the LESS important path to optimize.
what_the_proposal_misses.evidence: feedback.rs has 11 SignalTrigger variants. The implicit pipeline fires on every proactive_context call. The explicit pipeline fires only when a client explicitly calls /api/reinforce, which is rare.

what_the_proposal_misses.title: No discussion of the interference edge proposal
what_the_proposal_misses.detail: The proposal mentions 'On harmful: create interference edge' but the knowledge graph's edge types (graph_memory) don't include an 'interference' type. Adding a new edge type to GraphMemory requires schema changes, persistence format changes, and update to all graph traversal algorithms (spreading activation, Hebbian learning). This is a ~200-line change presented as a bullet point.
what_the_proposal_misses.evidence: No 'interference' edge type exists in graph_memory.rs.

what_the_proposal_gets_right: Per-memory Bayesian confidence tracking is the correct mechanism for per-memory adaptation — and it already exists.
The feedback taxonomy (helpful/irrelevant/harmful) maps cleanly to existing infrastructure (Helpful/Neutral/Misleading + review flag).
The insight that feedback momentum should affect the main retrieval pipeline, not just proactive surfacing, is correct — the disconnect is real.
The identification of feedback sparsity as a key risk is accurate.
blind_spots: The proposal doesn't acknowledge that hybrid_search.rs ALREADY has adaptive weight learning with proper safeguards (confidence ramp, max blend). It reinvents this in a weaker form.
No mention of the implicit feedback pipeline that already delivers high-volume signals to FeedbackMomentum.
No awareness that the 7-dim weights only affect proactive surfacing, not recall.
No consideration of A/B testing infrastructure (already exists: ab_testing.rs, handlers/ab_testing.rs) which could validate weight changes before applying them globally.

improvement_surfaces: Dual-weight-system incoherence (Coherence phi=6.2, Data Integrity phi=6.5)
Missing stability safeguards on global weights (Robustness phi=6.2)
Recall pipeline disconnected from weight learning
adversarial_reductions: E3 sparsity reduced from 5.8 to 5.5: proposal's explicit focus obscures the real need (connecting recall to existing implicit infrastructure)
dual_agent_divergence: Edge says plumbing is 80% done (FeedbackStore, momentum, Bayesian). Fractal says architecture is structurally broken (two weight systems is a design contradiction, not a gap).
blockers: Dual LearnedWeights must be unified before any new feedback pathway can deliver coherent weight adaptation

BIG.title: Unify LearnedWeights into single shared ScoringWeights
BIG.roi: Eliminates split-brain, enables any feedback path to affect all scoring. Prerequisite for all other improvements.

BIG.title: Attribution tracking in recall pipeline
BIG.roi: Makes weight updates directional instead of noise. Prerequisite for meaningful weight learning.

SMALL.title: Add confidence ramp to relevance.rs LearnedWeights
Momentum damping on weight updates (reuse FeedbackMomentum inertia model)
Extend /api/reinforce with 'harmful' outcome instead of new endpoint
Wire reinforce_feedback handler to call update_weights_from_feedback

FEATURE.title: Include per-memory attribution in recall response for client-side feedback

phase_2_5_avoidance_audit.coverage: BIG-1 addresses Coherence. BIG-2 addresses Data Integrity. SMALL-3+4 address Robustness. All gaps covered.

implementation_order.change: Add 'harmful' match arm to reinforce_feedback handler, mapping to Misleading + needs_review flag
Create unified ScoringWeights struct with both 7-dim proactive and 3-dim hybrid views sharing one Arc<RwLock<>>
Add effective_weights() with confidence ramp to the unified ScoringWeights, mirroring hybrid_search.rs:902 pattern
Add inertia-aware damping to weight updates using FeedbackMomentum's stability model
Capture per-dimension attribution in with_unified_score closure, return as RetrievalAttribution struct
After reinforce_recall in reinforce_feedback handler, call unified weight update with signal attribution
Add optional attribution field to RecallMemory response type, populate from BIG-2's RetrievalAttribution

modifications.modified: Call update_weights from handler after reinforce_recall returns — avoids threading through memory system boundary
modifications.reason: Simpler, fewer boundary crossings, same effect

interaction_risks: BIG-1 touches relevance.rs and hybrid_search.rs — same files as SMALL-3 and SMALL-4. Must sequence BIG-1 first.
BIG-2 produces RetrievalAttribution that FEATURE-7 exposes. Dependency chain.
SMALL-6 is independently valuable but reaches full potential only after BIG-1 unifies the weight target.
ordering_dependencies: SMALL-5 is independent — can go first
BIG-1 must precede SMALL-3 and SMALL-4 (they modify the type BIG-1 creates)
BIG-2 must precede FEATURE-7 (data producer before consumer)
SMALL-6 should follow BIG-1 for full effect but works partially without it
resource_conflicts: src/memory/recall.rs: BIG-2 + SMALL-6 (different functions, parallel OK)
src/handlers/recall.rs: SMALL-5 + SMALL-6 (different functions, parallel OK)
test_gaps: Need integration test: feedback → weight update → verify next recall uses updated weights
Need stability test: 100 random feedback signals don't collapse any weight below MIN_WEIGHT
Need convergence test: 50/50 helpful/misleading → verify weights stay near defaults

verdict.rationale: Do NOT implement as proposed. The proposal targets the wrong weight system (7-dim proactive weights that don't affect recall), creates API duplication (/api/feedback alongside existing /api/reinforce), and lacks the prerequisite infrastructure (unified weights, attribution tracking, stability safeguards). Instead, implement the Overloop-prescribed 7-step plan: (1) extend /api/reinforce with 'harmful', (2) unify the two LearnedWeights into shared ScoringWeights, (3) add confidence ramp, (4) add momentum damping, (5) add attribution tracking to recall, (6) wire reinforce to weight learning, (7) expose attribution in response. This achieves the proposal's stated goals without the architectural regressions.
blockers_before_implementation: BIG-1: Unify LearnedWeights (relevance.rs 7-dim + hybrid_search.rs 3-dim) into single ScoringWeights
BIG-2: Add attribution tracking to recall pipeline (currently discards per-dimension scores)
SMALL-3+4: Add confidence ramp and momentum damping to weight updates (currently no stability safeguards)
